This growth led to a notable expansion in net margin, rising 12.3% to 26.9%, while return on equity (ROE) declined 7.7% to 151.9%. Despite the slight decline in ROE, Apple's overall financial health appears robust, driven by its consistent revenue growth and expanding profitability.
